Long Island Expressway (I-495) is closed between Exit 52 and Exit 51 westbound due to flooding. NY State called to assist with pumping water.

Port Jefferson firehouse was flooded and had to be evacuated. There was also significant damage to their communication equipment. Numerous water rescues were performed in Port Jefferson with all water rescue assets utilized, including a high water rescue truck and two swift water rescue teams, for search and rescue of stranded motorists in flood waters. No injuries were reported.

The Nissequoque River dam broke around 9:30 am due to the flash flooding causing the entire Blydenburgh Pond to empty into the Nissequogue River. The river overflowed its banks sending water into homes and onto nearby streets. Two residents were trapped on the second floor of their home near the head of the River and had to be rescued by Smithtown Fire Department. The River flooding also impacted service on the Port Jefferson Line of the LIRR.
Water rescue on Miller Place-Yaphank Road.
Road closed at N Shore Road and 2nd House Road in Montauk due to flooding.
Water rescue on NY 25A at Mill Pond Road.
Multiple homes with basement flooding across NW Suffolk County within the cities of Commack, Smithtown, St James, Centereach, Setauket, Selden, Coram, Port Jefferson, and Rocky Point.
Car stuck in flood waters with occupant on roof of car on Middle Country Road at White Ave. Fire department responding.
Spotter reports FD responding to a flooded home on Bittersweet Avenue in Hampton Bays.
Road closed at Abrahams Landing Road and Cross Highway due to flooding.
Harbor Road collapsed due Mill Pond overflowing. Local FD began precautionary evacuations of nearby homes around 3:30am.
Both lanes of Hillside Road flooded in St James.
